Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (markedly elevated creatinine), proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color. There is also low HDL and borderline high LDL cholesterol. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended to clarify the etiology and guide management.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
